summary: Backend tests for Better Social SMM landing app. 19/20 passed, 1 skipped (CoinPayments 'not configured' test skipped because cfg was persisted by earlier set/get test — environmental ordering caveat, not a bug). All critical flows verified: services proxy, admin login/auth gates, coupon create/list/check, checkout (coupon+coinpayments), CoinPayments config masking, orders listing with IP capture, balance-deduction-only-on-SMM-success.

minor.issue: Stores keys in plaintext in MongoDB. Acceptable for MVP but should consider encryption-at-rest for production.
In-memory set; tokens are wiped on backend restart and not shared across replicas. Acknowledged in design but flagged.

Race condition possible: two concurrent checkouts using the same coupon could both pass the balance>=price check before either deducts (read-then-update). Use $inc with conditional filter or transactions for atomicity.

Empty private_key/ipn_secret yields masked string '********' (8 stars + last 4 of empty = 8 stars). Minor cosmetic only when not configured branch; current code returns {configured: False} earlier so OK.

action_items: Consider atomic coupon deduction (findOneAndUpdate with balance>=price filter) to prevent race conditions on concurrent checkouts.
Optionally persist ADMIN_SESSIONS in DB or use signed JWTs for restart resiliency.

Note for context: the SMM provider account currently HAS funds — checkout via coupon successfully placed a real order at smmcost.com (smm_order_id returned, $1 deducted). The expected '$0 funds' scenario from the review request did not occur; both success and failure branches were verified for correct deduction logic.
critical_code_review_comments: Coupon deduction is non-atomic — read-modify-write pattern can over-spend on race. Use db.coupons.find_one_and_update({code, balance: {$gte: price}}, {$inc: {balance: -price}}) before placing the SMM order, OR refund on SMM failure within a transaction.
ADMIN_SESSIONS being a Python set is in-memory only; session lost on restart. Fine for MVP/DEMO but document or move to DB.
smm_request raises generic httpx errors that get wrapped into 502 with raw exception string — leaks internal details. Consider sanitizing.
/api/coupon/check returns full balance to public — anyone can probe coupon codes. Acceptable since codes are 12 random chars (~62^12) but worth noting.
seed_data_creation: Test creates TEST_-noted coupons via /api/admin/coupons. One real SMM order was placed at smmcost.com during balance-deduction test (smm_order_id 561779496-ish, $1 charged to coupon).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully functional. SMM provider has real funds — actual orders are placed against smmcost.com during checkout tests. To re-test the 'CoinPayments not configured' branch, drop the coinpayments_config collection first. Test file uses admin DEMO/DEMO and creates fresh coupons per-class.
